questions.questionText: What does the top view of a glass (tumbler) look like?
questions.explanation: When you look at a glass from directly above, you see its round opening, which is a circle.
hints: Imagine looking down into a glass from above
What shape is the opening of a glass?

questions.questionText: The front view of a book standing on a shelf looks like a:
questions.explanation: When you look at a book from the front, you see its tall, flat cover, which is a rectangle.
hints: Look at a book standing on a shelf from the front
Is it taller than it is wide?

questions.questionText: Which 3D shape looks the same from the top, front, and side?
questions.explanation: A sphere is perfectly round, so it looks like a circle from the top, the front, and the side. No other common 3D shape looks the same from every direction.
hints: Which shape is round from every side?
Think of a ball

questions.questionText: What does a cone look like when seen from the top?
questions.explanation: From the top, you see the circular base of the cone, so it looks like a circle. From the front or side, a cone looks like a triangle.
hints: Imagine looking down at a birthday cap from above
What shape is the base of a cone?

questions.explanation: Rows go from left to right, like the lines in your notebook. Columns go from top to bottom, like pillars.
hints: Think of rows of desks in your classroom - they go side to side
Rows are like sleeping lines

questions.questionText: If a flower is at position C2 on a grid, what does C2 mean?
questions.explanation: C2 means Column C and Row 2. The letter tells you the column and the number tells you the row.
hints: Letters are for columns, numbers are for rows
C is the column, 2 is the row

questions.questionText: A map shows places as seen from:
questions.explanation: A map shows places as seen from above, like a bird flying over them. This is called a bird's-eye view or top view.
hints: Think of how a bird would see the ground while flying
Maps show the layout of an area

questions.questionText: On most maps, which direction is at the top?
questions.explanation: On most maps, North is at the top. South is at the bottom, East is to the right, and West is to the left.
hints: Think of the compass on a map
It starts with the letter N

questions.questionText: If you are facing North, which direction is on your right?
questions.explanation: When facing North, East is on your right, West is on your left, and South is behind you.
hints: Think of the compass: N-E-S-W going clockwise
The sun rises in this direction

questions.questionText: True or False: A cube looks like a square from the top, front, and side.
questions.explanation: True! All 6 faces of a cube are equal squares. So from the top, front, or side, a cube always looks like a square.
hints: Think about all the faces of a cube - what shape are they?

questions.questionText: What is the side view of a cylinder (like a tin can)?
questions.explanation: When you look at a cylinder from the side, you see a rectangle. The top and bottom edges are straight, and the height of the can forms the rectangle.
hints: Look at a tin can from the side - what shape do you see?
It has straight top, bottom, and sides

questions.questionText: On a grid, a cat is at A1 and a dog is at A3. They are in the same:
questions.explanation: Both A1 and A3 are in column A. The rows are different (1 and 3), but the column letter (A) is the same.
hints: Look at the letters - are they the same or different?
The letter tells you the column

questions.questionText: True or False: A photograph and a map show places in the same way.
questions.explanation: False! A photograph shows how a place looks from the front or side (the way your eyes see it). A map shows the same place from above (bird's-eye view). They look very different!
hints: Does a photo show the top view or the front view?

questions.questionText: The front view of a cone looks like a:
questions.explanation: From the front, a cone looks like a triangle. The pointed tip is at the top and the base is the wide bottom. From the top, though, it looks like a circle.
hints: Think of an ice cream cone from the front
It has a point at the top

questions.questionText: If East is to your right, which direction are you facing?
questions.explanation: If East is on your right side, you are facing North. When facing North: East is right, West is left, and South is behind.
hints: Use the compass: N-E-S-W clockwise
Which direction has East to its right?

questions.questionText: On a 5x5 grid, how many boxes are there in total?
questions.explanation: A 5x5 grid has 5 rows and 5 columns. Total boxes = 5 x 5 = 25 boxes.
hints: Multiply the number of rows by the number of columns
5 times 5 = ?

questions.questionText: Which object would look the same in its top view and front view?
questions.explanation: A cricket ball is a sphere, so it looks like a circle from the top AND the front. A book, tiffin box, and pencil all look different from the top versus the front.
hints: Which object is round from every direction?
Think about a perfectly round shape

questions.questionText: On a map, the hospital is to the North of the school. The park is to the East of the school. If you are at the school, in which direction will you walk to reach the park?
questions.explanation: The park is to the East of the school. So you walk East (to the right on the map) to reach the park from the school.
hints: Look at where the park is relative to the school
East is to the right on a map
